# Learning Domain-Driven Design (Vlad Khononov)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Analyzing Business Domains](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/01-analyzing-business-domains.md): The company's main area of activity and the overarching service it provides to its clients (e.g., retail, public transportation, coffeehouse chain).
    - [Discovering Domain Knowledge](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/02-discovering-domain-knowledge.md): The domain refers to the business-specific knowledge, logic, requirements, and workflows being modeled in software. It encompasses the mental models of domain experts, the business problems being solved, and the 'Ubiquitous Language' used to describe entities, behaviors, and invariants without technical jargon.
    - [Managing Domain Complexity](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/03-managing-domain-complexity.md): The business problem space or area of activity that is analyzed through subdomains and modeled into bounded contexts to manage complexity, define component boundaries, and resolve terminology conflicts during high-level software architecture design.
    - [Integrating Bounded Contexts](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/04-integrating-bounded-contexts.md): The communication, relationships, or integration contracts between multiple Bounded Contexts, subsystems, or microservices within a software architecture.
    - [Implementing Simple Business Logic](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/05-implementing-simple-business-logic.md): The design, refactoring, or implementation of simple business logic, specifically operating within supporting subdomains, generic subdomain integrations, ETL (extract-transform-load) processes, or basic CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations, where Transaction Script and Active Record architectural patterns are applied.
    - [Tackling Complex Business Logic](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/06-tackling-complex-business-logic.md): The domain represents the sphere of complex business logic, core subdomains, and tactical Domain-Driven Design (DDD) patterns, encompassing business rules, state transitions, and invariants that are modeled through a ubiquitous language and kept strictly decoupled from infrastructural or technological concerns.
    - [Modeling the Dimension of Time](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/07-modeling-the-dimension-of-time.md): Business logic for a core subdomain that requires deep data insights, rigorous audit logs, and historical state reconstruction (time traveling), specifically implemented through an Event-Sourced Domain Model where all state changes are expressed and persisted as immutable domain events.
    - [Architectural Patterns](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/08-architectural-patterns.md): Generating, refactoring, or analyzing the directory structure, module organization, system architecture, or dependency flow of a codebase, including requests involving Layered Architecture, Ports & Adapters (Hexagonal/Clean Architecture), Command-Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS), or the wiring of business logic to infrastructural concerns.
    - [Communication Patterns](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/09-communication-patterns.md): Designing, refactoring, or implementing communication flows between different system components, bounded contexts, or aggregates, including API integration, domain event publishing, asynchronous messaging orchestration, model translations (ACL/OHS), and management of long-running business transactions.
    - [Design Heuristics](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/10-design-heuristics.md): The domain refers to the scope of high-level software design decisions, including architecting system boundaries, sizing microservices or bounded contexts, selecting business logic implementation strategies, choosing structural architectural patterns, and defining testing strategies for specific software components or subdomains.
    - [Evolving Design Decisions](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/11-evolving-design-decisions.md): System refactoring, architectural modernization, migrating legacy code, adapting systems to organizational or business strategy changes, scaling existing bounded contexts, or addressing technical debt and 'big ball of mud' codebases.
    - [EventStorming](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/12-eventstorming.md): Facilitating, organizing, or simulating an EventStorming workshop; analyzing business processes or workflows through collaborative modeling; recovering lost domain knowledge for a legacy/brownfield system; identifying bounded contexts or aggregates from raw business requirements; or structuring domain events, commands, and policies for a software system.
    - [Domain-Driven Design in the Real World](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/13-domain-driven-design-in-the-real-world.md): The domain encompasses analyzing, refactoring, or modernizing brownfield projects, legacy systems, or "big ball of mud" codebases; decomposing monolithic architectures; introducing Domain-Driven Design (DDD) concepts into an existing codebase; and planning migration strategies for software systems.
    - [Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/14-microservices.md): The domain encompasses tasks related to designing, architecting, evaluating, or refactoring microservices, distributed systems, service boundaries, or APIs. This includes decomposing monoliths, defining service granularity, designing inter-service communication, and mapping domain-driven design (DDD) concepts (Aggregates, Bounded Contexts, Subdomains) to physical services.
    - [Event-Driven Architecture](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/15-event-driven-architecture.md): Designing, implementing, or refactoring inter-service communication, distributed systems architecture, microservice integration, event schemas, message brokers (e.g., Kafka, RabbitMQ), asynchronous workflows, or reviewing system coupling and resilience in an event-driven context.
    - [Data Mesh](.roo/vendor/architecture/learning-domain-driven-design-vlad-khononov/16-data-mesh.md): The design, architecture, or implementation of analytical data systems, online analytical processing (OLAP) models, business intelligence (BI) data pipelines, machine learning (ML) data feeds, or the transition from monolithic data architectures (Data Warehouses or Data Lakes) to a decentralized analytical architecture (Data Mesh).
